days.title: Arrival in the Region
items: Arrive in the region and pick up rental cars.
Pick up a local SIM/eSIM at the airport before heading out.
tips: Coverage in the southern Albanian mountains and on the Theth pass gets thin. Download offline maps for Albania, Montenegro and Bosnia while you still have wifi.

days.title: Arrival & the Stone City
days.drive: Arrival day, driving varies by route
items: Arrive in Tirana (TIA) and pick up rental cars.
Evening — Explore the Gjirokastër Old Bazaar.
Dinner options: Edua Gjirokaster (4-min walk) · Taverna Tradicionale Kardhashi (16-min walk) · Vojsava Restaurant (5-min walk).
Overnight in Gjirokastër.
tips: Gjirokastër is the UNESCO “City of Stone” — the bazaar is two cobbled streets, best at dusk when the day-trippers leave and the stone glows. Buy souvenirs here (carpets, hand-carved wood); prices beat the coast.
The old town is steep and cobbled. Wear real shoes, not flip-flops, even for a bazaar stroll.

days.title: Blue Eye & the Castle
days.drive: ~1 h 50 m / 72 km (base) → up to ~4 h 40 m / 221 km (with Përmet + canyon)
items: Breakfast at the villa (not included).
Drive to the Blue Eye (~36 km, 55 min). 2 km walk from parking to the spring; 20–30 min each way, 30–60 min to enjoy.
Castle of Gjirokastër (opens 9:00 AM, 15-min walk). Ramparts, Clock Tower, captured US jet ~1 h; add 1 h for the Armaments Museum & prison.
Lunch at Te Kalaja Restaurant & Rooms (4-min walk from castle) — strong vegetarian options.
Wander the Old Bazaar “stone city” streets (1–2 h).
OPTIONAL: Përmet — Llixhat e Bënjës thermal baths & Kadiut Bridge; Langarica Canyon light hike.
Dinner: Trifilia Slow Food (Përmet) or TBD in Gjirokastër.
Overnight in Gjirokastër.
tips: The Blue Eye pool water sits near 10°C year-round — stunning, but a brief dip only. Go early to beat both heat and crowds.
Thermal baths are open 24 h; early morning or evening avoids the midday crush. Bring your own towel, water shoes with grip, and a waterproof bag — facilities are minimal and rocks are slippery.
Decide the Përmet add-on the night before: it roughly triples the day's driving. Worth it for hot-springs lovers; skip if the group wants a slow castle day.

days.title: Coast Day — Sarandë
items: Breakfast, then check out of Gjirokastër.
St. George’s Monastery on Dema Hill — 15-min uphill walk; dual views of the Ionian Sea and Lake Butrint (1–1.5 h).
Drop luggage at the Sarandë hotel; head to the coast.
Mirror Beach (Plazhi i Pasqyrave) — pebbly cove, swimming & snorkeling. Arrive early afternoon to group sunbeds together. Backup: Pulëbardha Beach nearby.
Late lunch: Bar Restorant Pasqyrat (Mirror Beach) or Pulëbardha Bay & Restaurant.
Stroll the Sarandë Promenade; grab byrek at Peta Jon.
Sunset dinner at Lëkurësi Castle (hilltop, ~10-min drive). No reservation needed — they’ll find you a table. Sunset 8:01 PM; blue hour to ~8:32 PM.
Overnight in Sarandë.
tips: Every beach here is pebbles, not sand — water shoes are essential for all eight of you.

Open question from your notes: rooms may not be ready when you drop luggage. Ask the hotel to hold bags and use the beach restaurant / monastery facilities to change, or tip the front desk to let one room be used early.
Lëkurësi Castle at sunset is the day's showpiece — aim to be seated by ~7:30 PM to catch the light over Corfu.

days.title: Butrint & Ksamil Islands
items: Breakfast at the property.
Butrint National Archaeological Park — arrive 8:15 AM (opens 8:00). Amphitheater, baptistery, basilica, Roman baths; 2–3 h circuit.
OPTIONAL: Ali Pasha’s (Venetian Triangle) Castle by small boat from the Butrint car park.
Lunch at Abiori Restaurant Pizzeria, Ksamil — private beach, cabanas, clean changing rooms.
Afternoon: Ksamil Islands — four tiny islands by taxi-boat / “batela”. Swim, hike the paths, shade.
Dinner: Observatory (sunset views) · Bar Restaurant Limani (waterfront) · Green Restaurant (traditional / organic).
Overnight in Sarandë.
tips: Beat the Corfu ferry crowds: be at Butrint’s gate at opening. Tour buses roll in ~10:00 AM, so you get a quiet ~90-minute window.
Butrint sits between a lake and a channel — pack insect repellent for the shaded ruins.
Ksamil parking is a nightmare in July. Consider the Saranda–Butrint public bus, or park well back and walk in. Pre-agree a pickup time with your boatman.

days.title: Riviera to Dhërmi
items: Breakfast, then check out of Sarandë.
Waterfalls Mati, Borsh (~50–60 min / 36 km) — coffee at tables built over the water; ~45–60 min, almost no hiking.
OPTION 1 — Gjipe Beach: park near the Monastery of St. Theodore, 20–30 min hike down a red-earth trail. Closed-toe shoes for the trail.
OPTION 2 — Dhërmi public beach (where the hotel is). Lunch ~15 min north at Diego Bar Pizzeri.
OPTIONAL: Drymades Beach — rock formation splitting sandy and pebbly sides.
Sunset in Old Town Dhërmi — white stone houses, blue-domed churches. Drinks at Sofia Bar (terrace over the gorge).
Dinner at Taverna Fevan, Palasë/Dhërmi hilltop — family-run, authentic.
Overnight in Dhërmi.
tips: Don’t hike to Gjipe in flip-flops — the trail is loose and rocky. Carry beach shoes and switch at the bottom.
If the group is tired, Option 2 (Dhërmi beach at the hotel) is the low-effort win — no canyon hike, straight to the sea.

days.title: Berat & north to Shkodër
items: Leave Dhërmi no later than 7:30 AM (coastal route Dhërmi→Berat ~2 h 30 m / 159 km).
Berat — the “town of a thousand windows.” Berat Castle, Holy Trinity Church, Red Mosque ruins; cross Gorica Bridge for the Mangalem view.
Lunch: Eni Traditional Food · Taverna Lazaro · Antigoni.
ALTERNATIVE to Berat: Ujëvara e Peshturës (Progonat) — short 20–30 min rocky hike.
Drive Berat→Shkodër (~3–3.5 h). Check in, refresh.
Rozafa Castle at sunset (~8:07 PM) — panoramic views over Lake Shkodra and the Alps.
Dinner at Hotel Tradita — restored 17th-c. building, live traditional music, courtyard with fairy lights.
Overnight in Shkodër.
tips: This is the trip’s longest driving day. Split the wheel between the two families and pad every ETA — Albanian mountain roads run slower than the map suggests.
At Berat Castle, take a taxi up to the top gate and walk down — it saves the group’s legs for the cobbles.

days.title: Komani Lake / Shala River
items: Early start: 6:30 AM reservation with North Albania Boat. Meeting point: Shkoder Tourist Info (5-min walk).
Drive the bumpy road to the Komani Lake ferry.
Two-hour boat across Komani Lake — limestone cliffs and green water, “Albania’s fjords.” The Shala River tour includes the secluded river beach (no separate ferry ticket needed).
Return to Shkodër by early evening (~7:00 PM). Rest.
8:00 PM — Taste Home Shkoder (11-min walk): 3-course organic garden dinner, byrek and stuffed peppers.
Optional nightlife: Primo Lounge / ORO / Casablanca (cocktails) · Baza Pub / Budweiser Pub (lively) · n’Ode / M’Shijon (cozy).
Overnight in Shkodër.
tips: Notify the guesthouse in advance for vegetarian meals — fresh salads, cheeses, grilled veg and flija (layered pancake) are standard when asked ahead.
The road to the ferry is notoriously rough. Motion-sickness-prone travelers should medicate before boarding the van, not after.

days.title: Theth Valley
items: Depart by 7:00 AM. SH21 pass — newly paved but steep with switchbacks.
Buni i Bajraktarit viewpoint (1,700 m) — glass balcony over the valley. Breakfast at Qafe Thore.
Church of Theth & Lock-in Tower (Kulla e Ngujimit).
Grunas Waterfall — easy ~45-min hike each way.
Lunch at Villa Gjeçaj — organic slow food, standout vegetarian.
Blue Eye of Theth (Kaprre): drive to Ndërlysaj, then moderate 40-min hike each way to the turquoise pool. Best early afternoon for color.
Grunas Canyon viewpoint on the way back.
Return over the pass in daylight.
7:00 PM — Arti Zanave (women’s social enterprise, ~90% vegetarian). Alt: PURI or Genti.
Overnight in Shkodër.
tips: Trailheads (like the Blue Eye) are best reached by a local 4x4 even though the village road is paved — it saves an hour of rough walking.
Cross the pass back before dark. The switchbacks are unlit and the drop-offs are real.

days.title: Into Bosnia — Mostar
days.img: Mostar - Bosnia and Herzegovina - Stari Most.jpg
items: Start 7:00–7:30 AM — border crossings get busy in summer. Shkodër→Kravica ~5 h.
Kravica Waterfall — boardwalks and platforms, ~90 min, easy walking.
OPTIONAL: Počitelj Fortress — stone-paved open-air museum over the Neretva.
Blagaj Monastery (Tekija) from the outside — 30–45 min.
Check into the accommodation and drop off luggage.
Lunch at Food House Mostar (4-min walk from the bridge) — dedicated vegetarian platter.
Mostar Old Bridge (Stari Most, UNESCO) — view from the platform or terraced cafes; 1–1.5 h with the towers.
Old Bazaar (Kujundžiluk) — copper, carpets, crafts.
Dinner: Vrata Orijenta or Urban Taste of Orient. Nightcap: Night Bar Durazdzik.
Overnight in Mostar.
tips: Carry passports accessible and keep the rental car’s cross-border paperwork on hand — you clear two borders today. Confirm with your rental company that the car is authorized for Bosnia before you leave.
The famous bridge divers perform for tips; don’t let kids near the parapet edge — the drop to the Neretva is severe.

days.title: Montenegro Coast — Kotor
items: Breakfast, check out of Mostar.
Sveti Stefan viewpoint — cliff-top photo of the red-roofed island; short paved scenic walk.
Budva Old Town — marble pedestrian streets, Citadel, maritime museum. Lunch at Citadela Budva (fortress; group-friendly) or Pera Focaccia & Resto-Bar.
Scenic Budva→Kotor drive into the fjord-like bay.
Check into the accommodation.
Old Town Kotor (Stari Grad, ~15 min / 9.5 km) — car-free; park at Sea Gate / North Gate. Famous for its cats (Cat Museum).
Partial climb of the lower Kotor fortress walls for bay views (full route is 1,350 steps).
Dinner at Resto Bar Taraca (near North Gate) — Buddha bowls, vegan burgers. Alt: Ombra in the Old Town.
Overnight in Bay of Kotor.
tips: Kotor parking is brutal in summer. From the bay, drive to the North Gate lot early or taxi in — don’t circle the walls hunting for a space.
You only need the lower wall levels for the iconic bay shot; the full 1,350-step San Giovanni climb is optional and punishing in July heat.

days.title: Lovćen & Njeguši
days.drive: ~2 h 30 m / 89 km (base) → ~3 h 10 m / 128 km (with Cetinje + Lipa Cave)
items: Breakfast, then drive the Kotor Serpentine — 25 hairpins with the bay’s best land views. Start early, before the buses.
Lovćen National Park — Njegoš Mausoleum at Jezerski Vrh: lit tunnel, 461 steps, 360° Guvno viewing platform.

Njeguši village — stone houses and Konobas. Vegetarian feast of Njeguški cheese, honey, grilled veg, Balkan salads, Kačamak. Try Konoba Kod Pera Na Bukovicu, Etno Selo Sveti Georgije or Hotel Ivanov Konak.
OPTION 1A — Cetinje (old royal capital). OPTION 1B — Lipa Cave (60-min guided tour, 8–12°C, bring a light jacket).
OPTION 2 — Sunset cocktails at Forza Kuk or Monte 1350.
Dinner at Konoba Scala Santa, Kotor — pasta and vegetable risottos in the old town.
Overnight in Bay of Kotor.
tips: Reach the Njegoš Mausoleum before 10:00 AM — the summit lot is tiny and fills fast.
Njeguši is prosciutto country, but the Konobas do exceptional vegetarian dairy spreads — ask for the cheese-and-honey platter with warm bread.

days.title: Bay of Kotor by Boat
days.img: Our Lady of the Rocks, Perast, Montenegro.jpg
items: Breakfast at the accommodation.

Scenic boat tour from Kotor Old Town (Park Slobode), Tivat (Porto Montenegro) or a private Radovići/Luštica pickup. Blue Cave, Our Lady of the Rocks (Perast), submarine tunnels, Mamula Fortress. 2–4 h; start before 9:00 AM.
Lunch in Kotor (TBD).
OPTIONAL: Tivatska Solila nature reserve (former salt pans; 5 min / 2.7 km) — walking paths over the marshlands.
Pack for the return journey.
Dinner in Kotor (TBD).
Overnight in Bay of Kotor.
tips: August is Montenegro’s peak. Check the Kotor cruise-ship schedule and hit the Old Town on a low-ship day; start on the water before 9:00 AM to beat crowds and heat.
Our Lady of the Rocks is a man-made islet with a small church-museum — a quick, worthwhile stop even for non-swimmers.

days.title: Departure
items: Return rental cars at the airport.
Depart from Podgorica (TGD).
tips: Return rentals with a full tank and photograph the car at drop-off, cheap insurance against phantom damage charges.

meta.title: Albania, Bosnia & Montenegro
meta.subtitle: A 15-day Adriatic road trip
meta.summary: Stone cities, an Ionian coastline, fjord-green lakes and limestone alps — a self-drive loop from Gjirokastër to the Bay of Kotor.
